name: graphviz (Python Interface for Graphviz Graph Visualization)

auth.notes: Local library; graphviz system binary must be installed

performance.notes: Invokes graphviz (dot/neato/etc.) as subprocess; rendering time proportional to graph size

agent_readiness.notes: Python wrapper for Graphviz DOT language graph rendering. from graphviz import Digraph, Graph. dot = Digraph(comment='My Graph'). dot.node('A', 'Node A'). dot.node('B', 'Node B'). dot.edge('A', 'B', label='connects'). dot.render('output', format='png', cleanup=True). dot.view(). Attributes: dot.attr(rankdir='LR'). Node styles: shape, color, fillcolor. Edge styles: style, color, arrowhead. Subgraphs for clustering. Source: dot.source returns DOT string. Engines: dot, neato, fdp, sfdp, twopi, circo.

gotchas: Requires graphviz system package installed (brew install graphviz)
Limited layout control — Graphviz auto-layout algorithms not always predictable
SVG output may not render correctly in all browsers
Not the same as networkx — this is purely for rendering, not graph algorithms
best_when: Visualizing graph data structures, decision trees, or state machines
Generating DOT format graphs for documentation
Decision tree and tree data structure visualization
avoid_when: Interactive graph visualization (use pyvis or D3.js)
Very large graphs (use gephi or networkx layouts)
When graphviz system install is not feasible
summary: graphviz is the Python wrapper for Graphviz DOT language graph rendering. Digraph().node('A').edge('A','B').render(format='png'). Outputs: PNG, SVG, PDF, DOT. Node/edge styling (shape, color, label). Multiple layout engines (dot, neato, fdp). MIT. Requires graphviz system binary. Best for small-medium graph visualization in documentation and debugging. For interactive graphs use pyvis; for algorithms use networkx.
